description: V3 Ensemble voting and multi-timeframe confirmation configuration

layer_1_brain_voting.trim_justification: 10% trimmed mean is a standard robust location estimator (Wilcox 2012). With 21 brains, this drops the 2 most extreme predictions per class in each direction, reducing sensitivity to individual model failures while preserving ensemble diversity.

optional_weighting.notes: When enabled, weight each brain by its V2 evaluation metrics. Disabled initially — use equal weight until live performance data validates which metrics best predict forward performance. Premature weighting introduces selection bias.

layer_1_brain_voting.minimum_models_justification: Below 5 models, trimmed mean loses robustness. With trim=10%, 5 models trims 1 per side, leaving 3 for averaging. Below this, single model failures dominate.
layer_1_brain_voting.agreement_justification: 60% agreement means at least 13/21 models agree on direction. Random agreement would be ~33% (3-class). 60% is approximately 2 standard deviations above chance for a 21-model ensemble, providing statistical significance at p<0.05.

layer_2_timeframe_confirmation.primary_justification: M30 has the highest pass rate across all pairs (100% on EURUSD). It balances trade frequency with signal quality.

H1.justification: H1 models have the highest raw profit factors (up to 8.7). Required confirmation filters false M30 signals.

M15.justification: M15 at 71% pass rate provides timing granularity. Optional because not all pairs have strong M15 models.

M5.justification: M5 only viable at IBKR pricing. 80%+ pass rate on AUDUSD/GBPUSD. Provides precise entry timing when available.

layer_2_timeframe_confirmation.alignment_rule: Primary signal (M30) must align with H1 bias. If H1 disagrees directionally, degrade to FLAT. M15 and M5 are optional confirmations that increase position size confidence but do not veto.

layer_3_cross_pair_correlation.max_exposure_justification: Limiting to 3 correlated positions caps USD-beta at approximately 60% of total exposure. Beyond 3, drawdowns become correlated and diversification benefit collapses.
layer_3_cross_pair_correlation.rule: If more than max_correlated_exposure signals align in same USD direction, keep the top signals ranked by agreement_ratio and suppress the rest to FLAT.

tf_execution_thresholds.notes: A TF is allowed to execute only if it passes all 3 thresholds over the full replay window

layer_4_regime_gating.notes: Applied BEFORE inference. Hostile regime (REGIME_3) forces all signals to FLAT with no override. Low confidence (<0.6) degrades to conservative REGIME_2 policy.
